reasoning: The Lean signature captures each component of the informal claim: `[Field K] [Infinite K]` gives the infinite field, `[AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]` gives the vector space, `[Fintype ι]` with `γ : ι → Submodule K V` gives a finite family of subspaces, `h : ∀ i, γ i ≠ ⊤` expresses "each subspace is proper", and the conclusion `(⋃ i, (γ i : Set V)) ≠ ⊤` states that their union (as a subset of V) is not all of V (since `⊤ : Set V` is `Set.univ`). Encoding "for any finite family of proper subspaces, the union is not V" via universally quantified parameters γ and h is the standard equivalent formalization. The types, hypotheses, and conclusion all match the informal statement without adding or dropping any assumptions.
